articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Argentina cruise past Bolivia in World Cup qualifier
article.description: Argentina rested captain Lionel Messi but they still made light work of Bolivia as they won 3-0 to claim their second victory of the 2026 World Cup qualifiers on Tuesday.
article.linkText: Di Maria orchestrates Argentina win over Bolivia

article.story:
Argentina rested captain Lionel Messi but they still made light work of Bolivia as they won 3-0 to claim their second victory of the 2026 World Cup qualifiers on Tuesday.

Angel Di Maria took over as captain in Messi's absence and shone, providing two assists before half-time.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

"It's two games in, we knew it was important to get six points for what's to come," the 35-year-old Di Maria told Argentine broadcaster TyC Sports.

"To be handed the captain's armband by the best player in the world is unbelievable, I'm very proud."

Chelsea's Enzo Fernandez made it 1-0 after half an hour when he met Di Maria's cross with a close-range finish to score his third international goal.

"I'm a bit choked, the altitude feels a lot, but I adapted well," Fernandez said after his first match in La Paz, the world's highest capital city at 3,625 meters above sea level. "The game is like that. It's hard to catch my breath, but I felt very good."

Bolivia were dealt another blow five minutes later as they were reduced to 10 men after Roberto Fernandez was given a red card for an ugly tackle on Cristian Romero.

Nicolas Tagliafico doubled the lead and scored his first goal for World Cup holders Argentina with a header from a Di Maria free kick in the 42nd minute, before Nicolas Gonzalez's powerful strike sealed the win in the 83rd.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Roberto Fernández (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Gabriel Villamíl (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bolivia. Moisés Villarroel replaces Gabriel Villamíl.
Goal!  Bolivia 0, Argentina 1. Enzo Fernández (Argentina) left footed shot from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Ángel Di María.
Roberto Fernández (Bolivia) is shown the red card.
Goal!  Bolivia 0, Argentina 2. Nicolás Tagliafico (Argentina) with an attempt from the centre of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Ángel Di María   following a set piece situation.
Second Half begins Bolivia 0, Argentina 2.
Substitution, Bolivia. Luis Haquín replaces Adrián Jusino.
Substitution, Bolivia. Héctor Cuellar replaces Luciano Ursino.
Substitution, Bolivia. Fernando Saucedo replaces Jaime Arrascaita.
Jairo Quinteros (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bolivia. Carmelo Algarañaz replaces José Sagredo.
Substitution, Argentina. Exequiel Palacios replaces Rodrigo De Paul.
Goal!  Bolivia 0, Argentina 3. Nicolás González (Argentina) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Exequiel Palacios.
Substitution, Argentina. Leandro Paredes replaces Alexis Mac Allister.
Substitution, Argentina. Lautaro Martínez replaces Julián Álvarez.
Substitution, Argentina. Alejandro Garnacho replaces Nicolás González.
Carmelo Algarañaz (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Argentina. Ángel Correa replaces Enzo Fernández.
